source_citation: Seymour RS et al. (2013) J Exp Biol 216:3940-3948. PMID 23233168.
mass_source: Derived from species-specific allometric equation at representative mass 100 kg (within study range 0.19-389 kg)
notes: Measured SMR derived from species-specific allometric equation (Seymour et al. 2013): SMR=1.01*100^0.829=45.96 ml O2/min=2757.8 ml O2/h. Representative mass 100 kg used; mass is within the 0.19-389 kg range of the Seymour study. Baseline from clade-level reptile equation REP_BD76 (Bennett & Dawson 1976): 0.188*100000^0.80=1880 ml O2/h at 30°C. Temperatures match (both 30°C); no correction needed. Signed log residual = log10(2757.8/1880) = 0.1664. C. porosus SMR is ~47% above the reptile clade mean at this mass, consistent with active apex predator physiology. CAVEATS: (1) Measured value is from an equation fit to population data, not a single animal; (2) representative mass of 100 kg is a modeling choice within the valid range.

source_citation: Gienger CM et al. (2017) Ontogenetic comparisons of standard metabolism in three species of crocodilians. PLOS ONE 12(2):e0171082. doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0171082. PMC5300253.
mass_source: Derived from species-specific allometric equation (AM_GBT17) at representative mass 100 kg (within study range 1.29–104 kg)
notes: ACTIVE SOURCE: Gienger et al. 2017 (PLOS ONE 12(2):e0171082). Species-specific SMR equation for A. mississippiensis: SMR = 0.491 * M_kg^0.965 ml O2/min at 30°C. Animals fasted 3–4 days (post-absorptive). n=7. Study mass range 1.29–104 kg. Representative mass 100 kg used (within study range). Measured SMR from AM_GBT17 equation: 0.491 * 100^0.965 = 41.80 ml O2/min = 2508.1 ml O2/h. Baseline from clade-level reptile equation REP_BD76 (Bennett & Dawson 1976): 0.188 * 100000^0.80 = 1880.0 ml O2/h at 30°C. Temperatures match (both 30°C); no Q10/Arrhenius correction needed. signed_log_residual = log10(2508.1/1880.0) = 0.1254. Note: precise calculation gives 0.491 * 100^0.965 = 41.791 ml O2/min = 2507.5 ml O2/h → residual = 0.1251; prompt-specified rounded intermediates (41.80, 2508.1) give 0.1254; difference is 0.0003 (negligible). CAVEATS: (1) Measured value is from an allometric equation fit to population data, not a single paired animal measurement; (2) representative mass 100 kg is a modeling choice at the top of the study range. PROVENANCE: Prior record used AnAge database: resting MR = 0.1539 W at 1079 g at 22.2°C — non-computable due to temperature mismatch (22.2°C vs 30°C REP_BD76 reference). That value is retained in source_verification_matrix.csv as provenance (AnAge row, usable_for_delta_e=FALSE) but is not used for this computation.

source_citation: O'Connor TP (1999) Metabolic characteristics and body composition in Heterocephalus glaber. Comp Biochem Physiol A 124:147-152. PMID 10357434.
mass_source: Representative mass from O'Connor (1999); species mean ~30-80 g
notes: O'Connor (1999) measured BMR-equivalent rate for H. glaber at 30°C ambient temperature. Reported rate ~0.23 ml O2/min = 13.8 ml O2/h for ~35 g animal. CRITICAL ISSUE: H. glaber is a thermoconformer — an atypical mammal whose metabolic rate tracks ambient temperature rather than being regulated at a thermoneutral setpoint. The concept of a thermoneutral zone (TNZ) does not apply in the standard mammalian sense. Using MAM_KLB47 (Kleiber mammal baseline) as comparator: 607.2 * 0.035^0.75 = 607.2*0.0809 = 49.1 ml O2/h at 35 g. Hypothetical residual if computed: log10(13.8/49.1) = -0.552 (H. glaber ~72% below mammal clade mean by mass). This large negative residual likely reflects thermoconforming biology not evolutionary metabolic deviation in the sense intended by the delta_E framework. HUMAN METHOD DECISION REQUIRED: choose between (a) accepting this as a valid BMR measurement with FLAG_TNZ_UNCLEAR and computing the residual, acknowledging the interpretation caveat; or (b) classifying as non-computable due to absence of standard mammalian TNZ concept. Residuals are left null pending that decision.

source_citation: Southwood AL et al. (2007) J Exp Biol 210:1914-1924. PMID 17252517. Also: Wallace BP et al. (2005) J Exp Mar Biol Ecol 318:73-86; Paladino FV et al. (1990) Nature 344:858-860.
mass_source: Species mean body mass ~350 kg for adult females; Southwood 2007
notes: No whole-animal BMR or SMR exists for Dermochelys coriacea. The only available metabolic rate is diving metabolic rate (DMR) inferred from behavioral time-activity budgets: ~0.73 ml O2/kg/min (Southwood 2007). Unit note: 0.73 ml O2/kg/min * 350 kg * 60 min/h = 15330 ml O2/h total — but this is DMR, not SMR/BMR. DMR is explicitly classified as a non-compute physiological state. BIOLOGICAL CONTEXT: D. coriacea maintains elevated core temperature via gigantothermy (large body mass + counter-current heat exchangers in flippers and esophagus), not via elevated basal metabolic rate. Tissue-level metabolism is thermally independent (Paladino 1990, Davenport 1998), but whole-animal BMR in the reptile SMR sense is not measurable. Measurement is technically infeasible: requires controlled post-absorptive resting conditions for a 350+ kg obligate marine animal. IMPACT: Excludes D. coriacea from all quantitative delta_E analysis. Reduces target species N to maximum 4 even if all other species are resolved.

source_citation: Clark TD et al. (2008) J Fish Biol 72:1289-1298. PMID 17081787. Proxy for Thunnus thynnus.
mass_source: Species mean body mass ~250 kg for adult T. thynnus; Clark 2008 T. maccoyii individual masses not specified
notes: No direct Thunnus thynnus standardized metabolic rate exists. Clark et al. (2008) measured RMR for T. maccoyii (southern bluefin tuna, sister species) at 19°C in tunnel respirometer: 460 mg O2/kg/h. Unit conversion note (for documentation only): 460 mg O2/kg/h / 1.429 mg/ml * 250 kg / 1000 g/kg... units here are mass-specific; total rate for 250 kg animal = 460 mg/kg/h * 250 kg / 1.429 mg/ml = 80,500 ml O2/h — but this is proxy, non-standard state, and non-standard temperature, so it is recorded only. PROBLEMS: (1) FLAG_PROXY_SPECIES — T. maccoyii not T. thynnus; geographic range, thermal ecology, and metabolic physiology may differ; (2) FLAG_NON_STANDARD_STATE — RMR in a tunnel respirometer at minimum sustained swimming speed; tuna are obligate ram ventilators and cannot achieve true post-absorptive rest; (3) FLAG_TEMP_UNCORRECTED_REVIEW_REQUIRED — measurement at 19°C; no standard reference temperature for tuna and no Q10 correction authorized; (4) TUNN_CLK08_PROXY equation is marked verified=false in allometry_config.json. All four disqualifiers apply simultaneously. Impact: T. thynnus is non-computable under current data availability.

source_citation: AnAge: The Animal Ageing and Longevity Database. https://genomics.senescence.info/species/entry.php?species=Gallus_gallus
notes: AnAge reports BMR = 6.005 W at mass 2710 g. Unit conversion: 6.005 W * 179.1 ml O2/h/W = 1075.5 ml O2/h. Baseline from AVE_MW04 (McKechnie & Wolf 2004 all-bird equation): 0.8073 * 2710^0.669. Calculation: 2710^0.669 = 10^(0.669*log10(2710)) = 10^(0.669*3.4330) = 10^2.2969 = 198.1; predicted = 0.8073*198.1 = 159.9 ml O2/h. Signed log residual = log10(1075.5/159.9) = log10(6.726) = 0.8279. CAUTION: G. gallus at 2710 g is likely a domestic broiler or layer strain. McKechnie & Wolf (2004) baseline was calibrated on wild birds. Large positive residual (delta_E = 0.828, ~7× above avian mean) is likely attributable to artificial selection for rapid growth and high productivity rather than wild-type avian metabolic physiology. FLAG_ARTIFICIAL_SELECTION_RISK is mandatory. This species is included as a reference/outgroup; its elevated residual may not be biologically interpretable for the EME framework. Human review recommended before using G. gallus delta_E in comparative analysis.

source_citation: Selman C et al. (2001) Resting metabolic rate and morphology in mice (Mus musculus) selected for high and low food intake. J Exp Biol 204:777-784.
mass_source: Selman et al. 2001 C57BL/6 control group mean body mass
notes: Selman et al. (2001) reports RMR for C57BL/6J control mice at ~23°C: 0.34 W. Mass: ~26.5 g (0.0265 kg). Unit conversion: 0.34 W * 179.1 = 60.9 ml O2/h. Baseline from MAM_KLB47 (Kleiber 1947): 607.2 * 0.0265^0.75. Calculation: 0.0265^0.75 = 10^(0.75*log10(0.0265)) = 10^(0.75*(-1.5767)) = 10^(-1.1825) = 0.06562; predicted = 607.2*0.06562 = 39.85 ml O2/h, rounded to 39.9. Signed log residual = log10(60.9/39.9) = log10(1.526) = 0.1836. CAVEATS: (1) Physiological state is RMR (resting metabolic rate) not strict BMR (post-absorptive); RMR is typically 5-15% above BMR; (2) Measurement temperature 23°C is slightly below thermoneutral zone for mice (~26-34°C); cold stress at 23°C may elevate RMR above true thermoneutral BMR, inflating the positive residual; (3) C57BL/6 is a common laboratory strain; artificial selection effects on metabolic rate possible. FLAG_TNZ_UNCLEAR applied. This species is included as a reference/outgroup.
